 5 5titles

Page.title: Getting started in Confluence
Page.bodyStorage:
0b885b7f-2b42-41b1-8a5e-f0dc5e91cb27:Growth_purple:#EAE6FF🌱

Welcome to Confluence!

You can use Confluence to create a rich knowledge base or to collaborate with others using words, videos, images, graphics, charts, and graphs.

This page walks you through some Confluence basics like spaces, pages, and elements that allow you to create and manage impactful content for your teams.

Confluence 101

https://www.loom.com/share/5149671332234032989863f520d133ed

🗃️ Spaces

Spaces are where teams organize ideas, projects, and documentation. Spaces can be customized and integrated with both Atlassian tools and others.

Spaces can be organized with folders, and you can drag and drop to reorder or nest content so you can more effectively share your work with others.

More about spaces

16 incomplete Select+ Create at the top and choose Space to create a new space.

📄 Content types

Live docs let you collaborate in realtime with your team for better ideas, faster decisions, and unified edits – all without needing to publish.

Pages start as drafts that can be published into source-of-truth knowledge for your team — ideal for sharing policies, guides, and playbooks.

Whiteboards are your team’s place to brainstorm, build connections, and make ideas come to life.

Databases enable teams to build a library of information, track deliverables, and aggregate tools, links, and information from within and outside Confluence into a customizable view.

Smart links allow users to integrate and display content from other tools and platforms directly within Confluence, enhancing productivity by centralizing information in one accessible location.

More about live docs | More about pages | More about whiteboards | More about databases | More about smart links

 17 incomplete Select + Create button in the top navigation and choose content type to start creating content at any time.

Creating purposeful content

✏️ Real—time editing

You and up to 30 teammates can edit a page together - live - in real time. Changes save and sync automatically so that everyone editing sees the same thing.

More about live editing

🧰 Elements

Elements are tools like info panels, action items, and layouts, designed to help you make your pages more powerful.

More about elements
2283 incomplete Type/ to see a list of elements.

📏 Tables

Tables allow you to organize important information on a page to discuss it with your team.

Present the information the way it makes sense to you—resize columns, color cells, rows and columns, and sort the table by clicking the column headers.

More about tables
2284 incomplete Type/table to add a table at any time.

📸 Header images and emojis

Make your pages more visually impactful with header images and emojis.

🍕 Emojis aren’t just a fun way to spice up your page—they also show up in search, so using them strategically makes it easier to find pages later on.

More about page header images
2285 incomplete Hover over your page title and select Add header imageto customize your page.

💬Comments and mentions

Comments are a great way to start a conversation about a page or blog post. They allow you to remark on content, add important information, ask questions, and generally drive collaboration and teamwork.

@mention a teammate to bring them into the conversation, and they can reply to and/or like comments.

More about comments | More about mentions
25 incomplete Highlight text and select Comment in the pop up menu to leave an inline comment.

What’s next?

These are the top three actions we think will help you get the most from Confluence.

32 incomplete Explore a Space 33 incomplete Create a page 34 incomplete Invite a teammate

Jira ❤️ Confluence

Discover the integrations that help teams harness the combined powers of Jira and Confluence to ship projects faster.

 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ohtDFXNAUns

Back to top ⤴

Page.title: Explore Confluence Features
Page.bodyStorage:
Welcome to your trial, ! You have until to try all advanced features. With these features, you can bring clarity and control to your work.

22falsedefaultlisttrue

Get started with the top 3 most popular features 👇

Mission control Purplepremium

Access workspace insights and controls all in one place.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • See who has access to your Confluence workspace

  • Identify which spaces have the most inactive content

  • Manage user permissions and content privacy settings

  • Keep track of key workspace changes as users come and go and your company evolves

Helpful resources:

  • Act on insights from mission control

  • Feature highlights

  • Community page

https://www.loom.com/share/4cdf4dc46cc3402d869bf0706ff24083?sid=f51c6a41-0d21-4299-b34e-7842620c0b8e

Atlassian Intelligence Purplepremium

Summarize pages, get writing help, and more.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Draft, edit, translate content, improve writing, and change tone

  • Summarize pages, page comments, and blogs

  • Generate a summary or action items from a page

Helpful resources:

  • Get started with AI in Confluence

  • AI features in Confluence

  • Write or edit content

https://www.loom.com/share/0ac11ad9b11147bdaa9f7553490fa68b?sid=24c426e9-5d2f-43db-a337-c511faaf48d8

Page permissions Purplestandard Purplepremium

Control who can view, edit, and comment on pages.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Choose who can view or edit specific pages

  • Protect sensitive content

  • Share draft work with only a few collaborators

  • Lock down finalized documents to prevent changes

Helpful resources:

  • Manage permissions

  • Understanding permissions

  • Confluence permission structure

https://www.loom.com/share/b89cca91df7d4d6e8ebd8e6a54f04192?sid=76e03ffe-6327-4d84-9d57-cf3207400558

Productivity & collaboration features

Boost productivity and keep your team in sync with features designed to simplify collaboration and speed up work.

Unlimited users Purplestandard Purplepremium

Add anyone on your team, plus stakeholders.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Add team members without worrying about hitting a cap

  • Ensure that the right people at your company can see how work is progressing

Helpful resources:

  • How to invite your team

  • Collaborate on content

Public links Purplestandard Purplepremium

Share a read-only page with anyone.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Share read-only pages with contractors or stakeholders

  • Publish product FAQs or documentation externally

  • Reduce back-and-forth with support agents by making content self-serve

Helpful resources:

  • How to share with public links

  • Public links settings

  • Public links security

  • Community page

Team calendar Purplepremium

Plan deadlines and time off across teams.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Communicate availability and time off

  • Create and customize recurring events

  • See a birds-eye view of all time off across a team or company

  • Link events to related pages

Helpful resources:

  • Get started with team calendars

  • Feature highlights

  • Team calendar FAQ

Archive pages Purplestandard Purplepremium

Archive outdated pages to keep page trees organized.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Clean up outdated content without deleting it

  • Reduce search clutter and improve page relevance

  • Organize historical info for later reference

  • Make space navigation easier

Helpful resources:

  • Archive content

  • Page archiving

Guest access Purplestandard Purplepremium

Share projects and timelines with external clients.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Invite clients, vendors, or contractors to collaborate securely, for free

  • Limit access to only the spaces they need

  • Enable cross-org collaboration without overhead

Helpful resources:

  • Invite guests

  • What can guests see and do?

  • Assign space access to guests

Company hub Purplepremium

Build a single source of truth for all company info.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Centralize org-wide announcements and updates

  • Provide easy access to company resources and policies

  • Showcase culture and team highlights

  • Create a single starting point for employees

Helpful resources:

  • Create a company hub

  • Create a visually appealing hub

  • Community page

Unlimited whiteboards Purplepremium

Brainstorm or create flows on an infinite canvas.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Brainstorm ideas across teams without limits

  • Visualize project plans and workflows

  • Run team sessions, like brainstorming and planning, directly in Confluence

  • Move from sticky notes to structured pages with AI

Helpful resources:

  • Confluence whiteboards

  • Whiteboards plans and features

  • Community page

10x more automations Purplepremium

Automate time-consuming or repetitive tasks.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Trigger reminders for stale content or review cycles

  • Notify teams when a page is updated or published

  • Reduce repetitive tasks for admins

  • Scale automation with more monthly runs

Helpful resources:

  • Automations in action

  • Confluence automations

  • Create automation rules

  • Eliminate manual tasks

Bulk archive pages Purplepremium

Clean up projects by archiving more than one at a time.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Quickly clean up old project docs in one action

  • Tidy up spaces at the end of a sprint or quarter

  • Improve page discoverability

  • Keep knowledge fresh without manual cleanup

Helpful resources:

  • Clean a space with content manager

  • Archive a page

Admin control features

Stay in control with advanced admin features that help you protect content and manage communication with ease.

Announcement banner Purplepremium

Send critical messages to your team.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Broadcast company-wide updates at the top of every page

  • Share reminders about upcoming changes or downtime

  • Highlight important policy shifts or deadlines

  • Get visibility without spamming inboxes

Helpful resources:

  • Post Confluence-wide announcements in a banner

Admin key Purplepremium

Diagnose problems and make changes on any page.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Access restricted pages for troubleshooting

  • Make urgent updates on protected content

  • Reduce dependency on individual space admins

Helpful resources:

  • How to use admin key

  • Bypass page restrictions

  • Troubleshoot permissions fast

Sandbox Purplepremium

Test before making changes to your prod environment.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Test changes before rolling them out live

  • Train new users in a safe environment

  • Preview new features or templates

  • Validate permission settings and automation

Helpful resources:

  • Create a sandbox

  • What are sandboxes?

  • Test changes in a sandbox

Release tracks Purplepremium

Control when and how you release changes to users.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Schedule when updates roll out to your site

  • Give teams time to prepare for changes

  • Stay in control of feature adoption

Helpful resources:

  • What are release tracks?

Storage & support features

Scale confidently with storage that grows with you and support that’s always available.

Unlimited storage Purplepremium

Upload as many videos, images or files as you need.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Upload images, files, and attachments without limits

  • Centralize knowledge in one place

  • Avoid spending time on file cleanup

Helpful resources:

  • How to manage your storage

24/7 premium support Purplepremium

Get quick support directly from experts.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Get help at any time from experts at Atlassian

  • Resolve blockers fast with dedicated support

  • Reduce reliance on internal admins

  • Keep teams moving across time zones

Helpful resources:

  • Support services

99.9% SLA uptime Purplepremium

Ensure your services remain reliable.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Keep teams productive without interruptions

  • Reduce downtime risks during high-impact work

Helpful resources:

  • SLA breakdowns

Security & compliance features

Protect your data with advanced security and advanced admin controls, so your team can work confidently — no matter the scale.

Space permissions Purplestandard Purplepremium

Control who can view, edit, and comment on pages.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Control who can access or contribute to entire spaces

  • Give admin rights to space owners

  • Limit viewing of internal or confidential team spaces

  • Manage collaboration across departments

Helpful resources:

  • What are space permissions?

  • Assign space permissions

Copy space permissions Purplepremium

Copy permissions from one space to another.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Replicate permission structures for new spaces quickly

  • Set up consistent access across similar project spaces

  • Save time when onboarding new teams

  • Reduce manual errors in permission management

Helpful resources:

  • What are copy space permissions?

  • How to copy space permissions

Inspect permissions Purplepremium

Understand all active permissions for a specific user.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Quickly troubleshoot why a user can or can’t access content

  • Confirm content visibility before sharing

  • Audit and clean up unnecessary access

Helpful resources:

  • How to inspect permissions

IP allowlisting Purplepremium

Limit your site access to trusted IP ranges.

Learn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Limit Confluence access to company-managed networks

  • Enforce security for remote or hybrid teams

  • Protect sensitive data in high-compliance industries

  • Add flexibility with exceptions for trusted IPs

Helpful resources:

  • Specify an IP address

Data & insights features

Make informed decisions faster with built-in analytics that highlight engagement, track activity, and surface opportunities to improve.

Page insights Purplestandard Purplepremium

See engagement data, like page views.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • See who’s viewing or engaging with content (Premium)

  • Understand how often pages are accessed

  • Spot underused content or engagement drop-offs

  • Prioritize updates based on traffic

Helpful resources:

  • Page insights

Space analytics Purplepremium

Track user activity, product usage, and security.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Monitor site-wide activity and adoption

  • Identify inactive users or spaces

  • Track content creation trends

  • Make informed decisions on cleanup or permissions

Helpful resources:

  • View Confluence analytics

  • Watch how to use analytics and insights

Audit logs Purplestandard Purplepremium

View a list of all changes to your workspace.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Review changes to permissions, settings, and content

  • Track who made key updates for compliance

  • Investigate incidents quickly

  • Maintain a clear record of admin actions

Helpful resources:

  • View the audit log

Content manager Purplepremium

Oversee and manage all your content in one place.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Get a centralized view of all content

  • Filter and find outdated or underused pages

  • Take bulk actions like archive, delete, or update

  • Keep spaces clean and well-organized

Helpful resources:

  • Manage content at scale

  • How to use content manager

Ready to take the next step?

To choose the best plan for your team, simply click the trial button located in the top navigation.

Trial FAQ

Why am I on a trial?

When you signed up for Confluence, you were automatically given thirty days to try all paid features — absolutely free. At the end of your trial, you can choose a plan or downgrade to the Free plan.

Where can I add payment?

Error parsing storage content: 'You can add payment by clicking the trial button located in the top navigation, or by navigating to Billing & Manage Subscriptions in Admin Hub.'

Where can I see different plan options?

You can view all plans, features, and pricing structures on the Confluence page on the Atlassian website.

How long is my trial?

Error parsing storage content: 'Your trial is 30 days. You can see how long you have left in your trial in Admin Hub or by navigating to Billing & Manage Subscriptions.'

What happens when my trial is over?

If you don’t take action, your account will either be downgraded to the Free plan automatically or deactivated.

  • If you have less than 10 users, you’ll be downgraded.

  • If you have more than 10 users, you’ll be deactivated. We recommend upgrading to Standard or Premium if you have more than 10 users, since the Free plan limit is 10 users.

How do I extend my trial?

To have your cloud trial extended, please contact our Customer Advocate team with your site URL and we’d be happy to assist.

New trial licenses can be generated within my.atlassian.com. After logging in, select New Trial License, followed by the product you’d like to try. Please note, you’ll need your Server ID to generate a trial license key.

How do trials work across products?

Each Atlassian product (like Jira or Confluence) has its own trial. You can manage them separately or upgrade multiple products at once from your billing settings.

  • Error parsing storage content: 'You can get to billing settings by navigating to Billing & Manage Subscriptions in the Admin Hub.'

#EAE6FF

Is this guide useful? Want something different? Give us feedback here

Page.title: Template - Project plan
Page.bodyStorage:
Driver

@ mention the driver

Approver

@ approver

Contributors

@ contributors

Informed

@ stakeholders

Objective

Summarize the objective in 1-2 sentences

Due date

Type // to add a date

Key outcomes

List expected outcomes and success metrics

Status

not started / in progressYellow / completeGreen

 Problem statement

Describe the problem and its impact. Include the hypothesis that's driving your work ("We think X will do Y, and we'll know we've succeeded if Z").

 Scope

Must have:

  • Add your project's core requirements

Nice to have:

  • Add anything you want but don't strictly need

Not in scope:

  • Add anything you don't want to include

 Timeline

Add a timeline link here or type /whiteboard to create a timeline in whiteboards

 Milestones and deadlines

Milestone

Owner

Deadline

Status

e.g., Finalize designs for v1

@ owner

Type // to add a date


 Related links

Type /database to create a database of relevant research, meetings, or any other key documents for this project

Page.title: Template - Meeting notes
Page.bodyStorage:
Date

 Participants

  • Type @ name to mention a person and add them as a meeting attendee - they will be notified

 Goals

List goals for this meeting (e.g., Set design priorities for FY27)

 Brainstorm

Type /whiteboard to create an interactive canvas for icebreakers, brainstorms, diagramming, retros, and more

 Discussion topics

Time

Topic

Presenter

Notes

@ mention teammate

  • Add notes for each discussion topic





 Action items

incomplete

 Decisions

Type /decision to record the decisions you make in this meeting:

f4f93804-cb14-4fe4-a12c-a03aaac2bffa 8a83a27f-eb8e-4f9e-aa88-3950a986e7ee DECIDED

 Related info

Type /database to create a database of related information, meetings, or assets

Page.title: Template - Weekly status report
Page.bodyStorage:
Copy and paste this section for each week.

Type // to add a date

Win

  1. Add a few things that went well this week

Needs input

  1. Add some projects or efforts that need more input/help

 note

Focus

  1. Add some areas you'll be focusing on next week
\n

Focus

\n

Notes

Enter any other thoughts or feelings about this past week

Important Links

Add any key links that provide more information, e.g., KPIs, budgets, project status reports, etc.

Try it nowLearn More

How teams use this feature:

  • Get a centralized view of all content

  • Filter and find outdated or underused pages

  • Take bulk actions like archive, delete, or update

  • Keep spaces clean and well-organized

Helpful resources:

  • Manage content at scale

  • How to use content manager

Trial FAQ

Why am I on a trial?

When you signed up for Confluence, you were automatically given thirty days to try paid features — absolutely free. At the end of your trial, you can choose a plan or downgrade to the Free plan.

Where can I see different plan options?

You can view all plans, features, and pricing structures on the Confluence page on the Atlassian website.

How long is my trial?

Your trial is 30 days.

What happens when my trial is over?

If you don’t take action, your account will either be downgraded to the Free plan automatically or deactivated.

  • If you have less than 10 users, you’ll be downgraded.

  • If you have more than 10 users, you’ll be deactivated. We recommend upgrading to Standard or Premium if you have more than 10 users, since the Free plan limit is 10 users.

Welcome to Confluence!

You can use Confluence to create a rich knowledge base or to collaborate with others using words, videos, images, graphics, charts, and graphs.

This page walks you through some Confluence basics like spaces, pages, and elements that allow you to create and manage impactful content for your teams.

Confluence 101

https://www.loom.com/share/5149671332234032989863f520d133ed

🗃️ Spaces

Spaces are where teams organize ideas, projects, and documentation. Spaces can be customized and integrated with both Atlassian tools and others.

Spaces can be organized with folders, and you can drag and drop to reorder or nest content so you can more effectively share your work with others.

More about spaces

16 incomplete Select+ Create at the top and choose Space to create a new space.

📄 Content types

Live docs let you collaborate in realtime with your team for better ideas, faster decisions, and unified edits – all without needing to publish.

Pages start as drafts that can be published into source-of-truth knowledge for your team — ideal for sharing policies, guides, and playbooks.

Whiteboards are your team’s place to brainstorm, build connections, and make ideas come to life.

Databases enable teams to build a library of information, track deliverables, and aggregate tools, links, and information from within and outside Confluence into a customizable view.

Smart links allow users to integrate and display content from other tools and platforms directly within Confluence, enhancing productivity by centralizing information in one accessible location.

More about live docs | More about pages | More about whiteboards | More about databases | More about smart links

 17 incomplete Select + Create button in the top navigation and choose content type to start creating content at any time.

Creating purposeful content

✏️ Real—time editing

You and up to 30 teammates can edit a page together - live - in real time. Changes save and sync automatically so that everyone editing sees the same thing.

More about live editing

🧰 Elements

Elements are tools like info panels, action items, and layouts, designed to help you make your pages more powerful.

More about elements
2283 incomplete Type/ to see a list of elements.

📏 Tables

Tables allow you to organize important information on a page to discuss it with your team.

Present the information the way it makes sense to you—resize columns, color cells, rows and columns, and sort the table by clicking the column headers.

More about tables
2284 incomplete Type/table to add a table at any time.

📸 Header images and emojis

Make your pages more visually impactful with header images and emojis.

🍕 Emojis aren’t just a fun way to spice up your page—they also show up in search, so using them strategically makes it easier to find pages later on.

More about page header images
2285 incomplete Hover over your page title and select Add header imageto customize your page.

💬Comments and mentions

Comments are a great way to start a conversation about a page or blog post. They allow you to remark on content, add important information, ask questions, and generally drive collaboration and teamwork.

@mention a teammate to bring them into the conversation, and they can reply to and/or like comments.

More about comments | More about mentions
25 incomplete Highlight text and select Comment in the pop up menu to leave an inline comment.

What’s next?

These are the top three actions we think will help you get the most from Confluence.

32 incomplete Explore a Space 33 incomplete Create a page 34 incomplete Invite a teammate

Jira ❤️ Confluence

Discover the integrations that help teams harness the combined powers of Jira and Confluence to ship projects faster.

 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ohtDFXNAUns

Back to top ⤴

Ready to take the next step?

To choose the best plan for your team, simply click the trial button located in the top navigation.

Trial FAQ

Why am I on a trial?

When you signed up for Confluence, you were automatically given thirty days to try all paid features — absolutely free. At the end of your trial, you can choose a plan or downgrade to the Free plan.

Where can I add payment?

Error parsing storage content: 'You can add payment by clicking the trial button located in the top navigation, or by navigating to Billing & Manage Subscriptions in Admin Hub.'

Where can I see different plan options?

You can view all plans, features, and pricing structures on the Confluence page on the Atlassian website.

How long is my trial?

Error parsing storage content: 'Your trial is 30 days. You can see how long you have left in your trial in Admin Hub or by navigating to Billing & Manage Subscriptions.'

What happens when my trial is over?

If you don’t take action, your account will either be downgraded to the Free plan automatically or deactivated.

  • If you have less than 10 users, you’ll be downgraded.

  • If you have more than 10 users, you’ll be deactivated. We recommend upgrading to Standard or Premium if you have more than 10 users, since the Free plan limit is 10 users.

How do I extend my trial?

To have your cloud trial extended, please contact our Customer Advocate team with your site URL and we’d be happy to assist.

New trial licenses can be generated within my.atlassian.com. After logging in, select New Trial License, followed by the product you’d like to try. Please note, you’ll need your Server ID to generate a trial license key.

How do trials work across products?

Each Atlassian product (like Jira or Confluence) has its own trial. You can manage them separately or upgrade multiple products at once from your billing settings.

  • Error parsing storage content: 'You can get to billing settings by navigating to Billing & Manage Subscriptions in the Admin Hub.'

Trial FAQ

Why am I on a trial?

When you signed up for Confluence, you were automatically given thirty days to try paid features — absolutely free. At the end of your trial, you can choose a plan or downgrade to the Free plan.

Where can I see different plan options?

You can view all plans, features, and pricing structures on the Confluence page on the Atlassian website.

How long is my trial?

Your trial is 30 days.

What happens when my trial is over?

If you don’t take action, your account will either be downgraded to the Free plan automatically or deactivated.

  • If you have less than 10 users, you’ll be downgraded.

  • If you have more than 10 users, you’ll be deactivated. We recommend upgrading to Standard or Premium if you have more than 10 users, since the Free plan limit is 10 users.
